definition: Immigration operates as a credit creation channel, not merely a labor supply channel, generating approximately $90–120B in new mortgage origination annually through housing formation and consumption-driven borrowing.
evidence: Estimated $90–120B in new mortgage origination annually from immigration
Operates through housing formation and consumption-driven borrowing
open_questions: Whether credit creation from immigration is a first-order or second-order effect
How immigration restriction affects aggregate credit growth
